name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its simple and readable syntax. It's widely used in web development, automation, data science, and education. Its extensive libraries and active community make it an ideal choice for beginners and professionals alike.
usedFor: Web development (Django, Flask), data science (pandas, NumPy), machine learning (TensorFlow, PyTorch), automation, scripting, and education.
funFact: Python is named after the comedy group Monty Python, not the snake.

curriculum.title: Hello, Python!
curriculum.objective: Understand how to write and run your first Python program.
curriculum.explanation: Python programs can be run interactively in a terminal or saved in a file to run. The simplest program prints text using the print() function.

keyConcepts.definition: A function that outputs text to the screen.
A sequence of characters in quotes, like 'Hello'.

curriculum.challenge: Write a Python program that prints your name.
curriculum.hint: Change the text inside the print() function's quotes.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Don’t worry about memorizing everything. Experiment and ask questions as you go!

curriculum.title: Variables and Numbers
curriculum.objective: Learn how to use variables to store numbers in Python.
curriculum.explanation: Variables are like containers that hold data. You assign a value with = and can then use the variable's name to refer to it.

keyConcepts.definition: A named storage for data.
Giving a variable a value with =.

curriculum.challenge: Create a variable named height and set it to 170. Print its value.
curriculum.hint: Use = to assign height and print(height) to display it.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Use clear and descriptive variable names for readability.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript is a versatile scripting language primarily used for interactive web pages. It runs in all modern web browsers and powers client-side logic for most websites. JavaScript is also used on the server through platforms like Node.js.
usedFor: Front-end web development, interactive UIs, web apps, server-side scripts (Node.js), and mobile apps.
funFact: JavaScript was created in just 10 days.

curriculum.title: Your First JavaScript
curriculum.objective: Learn how to output text using JavaScript.
curriculum.explanation: JavaScript runs in browsers. The simplest way to see output is with console.log(), which writes messages to the developer console.

keyConcepts.definition: A function that prints output to the browser's console.
Text written in single or double quotes.

curriculum.challenge: Print your favorite color using JavaScript.
curriculum.hint: Change the text inside console.log().
curriculum.beginnerTip: Use the browser's console to see your JavaScript output instantly.

curriculum.title: Variables and Data Types
curriculum.objective: Understand variables and simple data types in JavaScript.
curriculum.explanation: You declare variables using let, const, or var. They can hold numbers, strings, or other data.

keyConcepts.definition: Used to declare a block-scoped variable.
Declares a constant variable whose value can't be changed.

curriculum.challenge: Declare a variable called city with your hometown as its value and print it.
curriculum.hint: Use let or const, assignment =, and console.log.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Use let for values that change, const for constants.

name: TypeScript
creator: Microsoft
description: TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that adds static typing and compile-time checks. It helps developers write safer and more maintainable code, especially for large projects. TypeScript compiles down to standard JavaScript.
usedFor: Large-scale web apps, frontend frameworks (Angular, React, Vue), and Node.js development.
funFact: TypeScript was designed to address JavaScript’s weaknesses in large codebases.

curriculum.title: TypeScript Basics
curriculum.objective: Understand how TypeScript relates to JavaScript.
curriculum.explanation: TypeScript extends JavaScript by adding types. This means you must declare what kind of values variables can hold, which helps avoid errors.

keyConcepts.definition: Specifies what type of value a variable can hold.
Converting TypeScript to JavaScript to run in browsers.

curriculum.challenge: Declare a variable called username that can only be a string. Assign your name.
curriculum.hint: Use : string after the variable name.
curriculum.beginnerTip: TypeScript code won't run directly in browsers; it must be compiled first.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: C is one of the oldest and most influential programming languages, known for its performance and low-level hardware access. It is used to build operating systems, embedded software, and high-speed applications. Many modern languages are directly inspired by C.
usedFor: Operating systems, embedded systems, device drivers, performance-critical applications.
funFact: The Unix operating system was originally written in C.

curriculum.title: Hello, C
curriculum.objective: Write and understand your first C program.
curriculum.explanation: Every C program starts with a main() function. You use printf() to output text. Don’t forget the semicolon at the end of each statement.

keyConcepts.definition: The entry point for every C program.
Function to print text to the screen.

curriculum.challenge: Print your favorite number using printf().
curriculum.hint: Replace the text in printf with your number.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Don’t worry about memorizing #include. Focus on writing simple programs first.

name: C++
creator: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: C++ builds upon C by adding object-oriented programming and other powerful features. It's used for performance-intensive applications where control over resources is essential. C++ remains the backbone of many large software systems.
usedFor: Game engines, desktop applications, financial systems, real-time systems, and embedded software.
funFact: C++ originally started as 'C with Classes'.

curriculum.title: C++ Hello World
curriculum.objective: Learn to write and run your first C++ program.
curriculum.explanation: Like C, C++ programs start at main(), but output uses std::cout from the iostream library.

keyConcepts.definition: Outputs text to the console in C++.

curriculum.challenge: Output the number 42 in C++.
curriculum.hint: Replace the text in std::cout with your number.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Learning the basics of C before C++ may help, but is not mandatory.

name: C#
creator: Microsoft
description: C# is a modern, object-oriented programming language developed by Microsoft. It's commonly used for building Windows applications, backend services, and games through the Unity engine. Its syntax is similar to other C-family languages.
usedFor: Windows applications, web APIs, backend services, Unity game development.
funFact: C# is pronounced 'C-sharp' and the name comes from musical notation.

curriculum.title: C# First Program
curriculum.objective: Write a simple C# console application.
curriculum.explanation: The Main() method is the entry point of every C# program. Use Console.WriteLine to print to the terminal.

keyConcepts.definition: Prints text to the terminal in C#.

curriculum.challenge: Print the phrase "I love programming" in C#.
curriculum.hint: Use Console.WriteLine with your own message.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Start with console apps before moving to graphical or web-based ones.

name: Rust
creator: Graydon Hoare
description: Rust is a systems programming language emphasizing safety and performance without a garbage collector. It prevents common bugs like memory leaks, making it popular for high-reliability software and systems.
usedFor: Systems programming, web assembly, command-line tools, high-performance services.
funFact: Rust’s mascot is a crab named Ferris.

curriculum.title: Hello, Rust!
curriculum.objective: Write and run your first Rust program.
curriculum.explanation: Rust programs start with a main function and use println! for output. The exclamation mark means it's a macro, not a regular function.

keyConcepts.definition: The entry point for every Rust program.
Prints text to the terminal.

curriculum.challenge: Print the number 2024.
curriculum.hint: Change the string inside println!().
curriculum.beginnerTip: Don’t worry about ownership at first. Focus on writing and running simple programs.

name: Go
creator: Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, Ken Thompson
description: Go (or Golang) is a statically typed programming language developed at Google. It is known for its simplicity, fast compilation, and built-in support for concurrency. Go is often used for cloud services and performant backend systems.
usedFor: Web servers, cloud infrastructure, microservices, command-line tools.
funFact: Go’s mascot is a blue gopher.

curriculum.title: Go Basics
curriculum.objective: Run your first Go program.
curriculum.explanation: Every Go program begins with a package main and a main() function. Use fmt.Println() to print to the console.

keyConcepts.definition: The starting package for executable Go programs.
Prints output to the console.

curriculum.challenge: Print your age using Go.
curriculum.hint: Change the string in fmt.Println().
curriculum.beginnerTip: Keep your code organized—Go uses strict rules about formatting.

name: Java
creator: James Gosling
description: Java is a class-based, object-oriented language designed for portability and reliability. It's widely used for enterprise software, Android apps, and backend servers. The Java Virtual Machine allows Java to run on many platforms.
usedFor: Android apps, web servers, enterprise software, large systems.
funFact: Java’s original name was Oak.

curriculum.title: Hello, Java!
curriculum.objective: Write and run your first Java program.
curriculum.explanation: All Java code lives inside a class. The main method is the starting point and System.out.println displays output.

keyConcepts.definition: A blueprint for objects and code structure in Java.
The entry point method for Java programs.

curriculum.challenge: Output your birth year in Java.
curriculum.hint: Change the text inside System.out.println.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Java code must always be inside a class and follow strict syntax.

name: Assembly
creator: Kathleen Booth and others
description: Assembly language refers to low-level programming languages that are closely related to machine code. Each processor family has its own assembly language. It's mainly used to write very efficient code and to interact directly with hardware.
usedFor: Embedded programming, device drivers, firmware, reverse engineering.
funFact: Every instruction in assembly corresponds directly to a machine code operation.

curriculum.title: Assembly: The Basics
curriculum.objective: Learn how an assembly program is structured.
curriculum.explanation: Assembly instructions are simple tasks performed by the CPU. Programs vary by processor, but simple tasks like moving data and displaying output are common.

keyConcepts.definition: A single command for the processor.

curriculum.codeExample:
section .data
msg db 'Hello, world!',0xA
section .text
global _start
_start:
  mov edx,13
  mov ecx,msg
  mov ebx,1
  mov eax,4
  int 0x80
  mov eax,1
  int 0x80
curriculum.challenge: Change the string to print your name using db.
curriculum.hint: Replace 'Hello, world!' with your name.
curriculum.solution:
section .data
msg db 'Alex',0xA
section .text
global _start
_start:
  mov edx,5
  mov ecx,msg
  mov ebx,1
  mov eax,4
  int 0x80
  mov eax,1
  int 0x80
curriculum.beginnerTip: Expect to do lots of research—assembly language is different for every processor.

name: BASIC
creator: John G. Kemeny, Thomas E. Kurtz
description: BASIC stands for Beginner's All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code. It was designed to make programming straightforward and accessible, especially for students and hobbyists. Many early home computers used BASIC as their main language.
usedFor: Early home computing, simple games, hobby programming, education.
funFact: BASIC was one of the first languages meant for non-engineers.

curriculum.title: Getting Started with BASIC
curriculum.objective: Print your first message in BASIC.
curriculum.explanation: BASIC uses simple numbered lines and keywords to perform tasks. PRINT is used for output.

keyConcepts.definition: Outputs text to the screen in BASIC.

curriculum.challenge: Modify the statement to print your name.
curriculum.hint: Change the text inside the quotes after PRINT.
curriculum.beginnerTip: BASIC is easy to read—line numbers keep your code organized.

name: Lua
creator: Roberto Ierusalimschy, Luiz Henrique de Figueiredo, Waldemar Celes
description: Lua is a lightweight, fast scripting language often used for embedding in applications. It's widely used in game development for scripting game logic. Lua's syntax is simple and flexible.
usedFor: Game scripting, embedded systems, extending software.
funFact: Lua means 'moon' in Portuguese.

curriculum.title: Lua Hello World
curriculum.objective: Output your first message using Lua.
curriculum.explanation: Lua uses the print function for displaying output. Statements end at the line's end—no semicolons are needed.

keyConcepts.definition: Function to display output in Lua.

curriculum.challenge: Print your favorite animal using Lua.
curriculum.hint: Replace the message in print("...").
curriculum.beginnerTip: Try typing and running code in an interactive Lua prompt to see instant results.

name: Ruby
creator: Yukihiro Matsumoto
description: Ruby is a dynamic, interpreted programming language that focuses on simplicity and productivity. Its human-readable syntax makes it easy to learn. Ruby is especially well-known for powering the Ruby on Rails web framework.
usedFor: Web applications, scripting, automation, DevOps.
funFact: Ruby was designed for developer happiness and productivity.

curriculum.title: First Steps in Ruby
curriculum.objective: Learn how to print text in Ruby.
curriculum.explanation: Ruby’s print statements are very straightforward. Use puts to print a line with a newline at the end.

keyConcepts.definition: A command that prints a string followed by a new line.

curriculum.challenge: Print the phrase 'Good luck!' in Ruby.
curriculum.hint: Replace the text in double quotes and use puts.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Read Ruby code aloud—it often reads like English sentences!

name: Swift
creator: Apple Inc.
description: Swift is a safe, fast programming language developed by Apple for iOS, macOS, and related platforms. It combines the power of compiled languages with an approachable syntax. Swift is now open-source and rapidly growing.
usedFor: iOS and macOS app development, server-side development.
funFact: Swift was designed to be safer than Objective-C and includes modern language features.

curriculum.title: Start with Swift
curriculum.objective: Output text using print in Swift.
curriculum.explanation: The print function shows output in Swift just like many other languages. Statements don't need semicolons.

keyConcepts.definition: Outputs text to the console in Swift.

curriculum.challenge: Print your favorite fruit in Swift.
curriculum.hint: Type your fruit inside the quotes in print().
curriculum.beginnerTip: Use Xcode's playgrounds to experiment with Swift code interactively.

name: Kotlin
creator: JetBrains
description: Kotlin is a modern, expressive programming language officially supported for Android app development. Its concise syntax and null-safety features make it a safer alternative to Java.
usedFor: Android app development, server-side programming, multiplatform applications.
funFact: Kotlin is named after an island near St. Petersburg, Russia.

curriculum.title: Kotlin Basics
curriculum.objective: Write a simple program that prints a message.
curriculum.explanation: In Kotlin, the main function marks the entry point. Use println() to print output.

keyConcepts.definition: Defines the starting function for Kotlin apps.

curriculum.challenge: Print your favorite city in Kotlin.
curriculum.hint: Type your city in quotes inside println().
curriculum.beginnerTip: Practice in Kotlin playground to learn by doing.

name: PHP
creator: Rasmus Lerdorf
description: PHP is a widely-used scripting language especially suited for web development. It can generate dynamic page content and is executed on the server side. PHP is one of the core languages of the web.
usedFor: Server-side web applications, content management systems, blogs, web APIs.
funFact: Over 75% of all websites use PHP in some capacity.

curriculum.title: PHP: Getting Started
curriculum.objective: Write and run your first PHP script.
curriculum.explanation: PHP code is embedded inside special <?php ... ?> tags. Use echo to display output.

keyConcepts.definition: Outputs text in PHP.

curriculum.challenge: Change the message to display your school name.
curriculum.hint: Type your school name in quotes after echo.
curriculum.beginnerTip: PHP is typically run on a web server, but many tools let you run code locally.

name: Bash
creator: Brian Fox
description: Bash is a Unix shell and command language for interacting with operating systems. It's popular for automating tasks and scripting processes on Linux and macOS.
usedFor: Automating system tasks, installing software, scripting, DevOps, system administration.
funFact: Bash stands for Bourne Again SHell, referencing the original Bourne Shell.

curriculum.title: Hello Bash
curriculum.objective: Write your first Bash script to print a message.
curriculum.explanation: Use the echo command in a .sh file or terminal to print text.

keyConcepts.definition: Command to print text in Bash.

curriculum.challenge: Display your username using echo.
curriculum.hint: Type your username inside the quotes.
curriculum.beginnerTip: You can write and run small scripts directly in the terminal!

name: R
creator: Ross Ihaka, Robert Gentleman
description: R is a programming language for statistical computing and graphics. It is widely used in data analysis, visualization, and research in science and academia.
usedFor: Data analysis, visualization, statistics, data science, academic research.
funFact: R was named after its first two developers, Ross and Robert.

curriculum.title: R: Print Basics
curriculum.objective: Display messages in R using the print function.
curriculum.explanation: The print() function displays output. You can print numbers or text placed in quotes.

keyConcepts.definition: Displays output in R.

curriculum.challenge: Print the number 10 in R.
curriculum.hint: You can print numbers without quotes.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Try out R code in RStudio or an online R interpreter.

name: Dart
creator: Lars Bak and the Dart team at Google
description: Dart is a programming language designed for client development, especially for web and mobile apps with Flutter. It’s optimized for UI, offering fast compilation and developer-friendly tools.
usedFor: Mobile apps (with Flutter), web applications, server-side development.
funFact: Dart is the primary language for building Flutter mobile apps.

curriculum.title: Your First Dart Program
curriculum.objective: Print a message using Dart.
curriculum.explanation: The main() function is where code execution begins. Use print() to display output.

keyConcepts.definition: Entry point for Dart applications.
Function to print output in Dart.

curriculum.challenge: Print your favorite movie title in Dart.
curriculum.hint: Replace 'Hello, Dart!' with your movie title inside print().
curriculum.beginnerTip: Use DartPad online to run and test Dart code instantly.

name: Scala
creator: Martin Odersky
description: Scala combines object-oriented and functional programming on the JVM. It's popular in data engineering for its expressiveness and compatibility with big data tools.
usedFor: Big data processing, web servers, concurrent applications, data science.
funFact: Scala stands for 'scalable language', designed for growing projects.

curriculum.title: Scala Fundamentals
curriculum.objective: Print your first message in Scala.
curriculum.explanation: Scala uses println to display output. The main method marks where the program begins.

keyConcepts.definition: A singleton instance often used for the entry point of Scala apps.
Function to print output in Scala.

curriculum.challenge: Output your favorite language in Scala.
curriculum.hint: Change the text inside println to your answer.
curriculum.beginnerTip: Scala code can be run in a REPL to try concepts interactively.
